DECLARATION OF COUNSEL IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 76057602) FILED BY DEFENDANT AVILES-LANDAU, CYNTHIA THE ESTATE OF THELMA B. AVILES

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND ON GROUNDS OF UNCERTAINTY AND FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 30 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT CLEARLY AND PROPERLY LABELING EACH CAUSE OF ACTION (FOR EXAMPLE, COUNTS 7 AND 8 DO NOT STATE A CLAIM FOR RELIEF) AND WHICH DEFENDANT EACH CAUSE OF ACTION IS ASSERTED AGAINST, EITHER CYNTHIA AVILES-LANDAU INDIVIDUALLY, OR IN HER CAPACITY AS ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE, OR BOTH. FURTHERMORE, TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF'S INTENT IS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, PLAINTIFF MUST PROPERLY LABEL THE CAUSE OF ACTION AND ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, SUPPORTING EACH ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF IS ON NOTICE THAT IT CANNOT AMEND THE COMPLAINT AND ADD NEW CAUSES OF ACTION AFTER A DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHOUT OBTAINING PERMISSION TO DO SO. (COMMUNITY WATER COALITION V. SANTA CRUZ COUNTY LOCAL AGENCY FORMATION COM. (2011) 200 CAL.APP.4TH 1317, 1329.) THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RECEIPT OF STOLEN PROPERTY AND EXTORTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE PROPERTY AT ISSUE, I.E., 331 POPE STREET, WAS LAWFULLY OBTAINED THROUGH UNLAWFUL DETAINER PROCEEDINGS, CASE NO. CUD-24-674073. THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE 17200, IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF CANNOT SHOW THAT "MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC ARE LIKELY TO BE DECEIVED" BASED ON THE ISOLATED INCIDENT THAT DEALT WITH EVICTION PROCEEDINGS. (SEE, KOREA SUPPLY CO. V, LOCKHEED MATIN CORP. (2003) 29 CAL.4TH 1134, 1151.) **PLEASE SEE SCANED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING** ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: VICTOR M. HWANG ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

TENTATIVE RULING: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 8, 2026. Line 1.

DEFENDANT CYNTHIA AVILES-LANDAU, THE ESTATE OF THELMA B. AVILES DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

is CONTINUED to July 9, 2026 on the Court's own motion. =(501/CFH)

Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT CYNTHIA AVILES-LANDAU, THE ESTATE OF THELMA B. AVILES DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend on grounds of uncertainty and for failure to state a cause of action. Plaintiff shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint clearly and properly labeling each cause of action (for example, Counts 7 and 8 do not state a claim for relief) and which defendant each cause of action is asserted against, either Cynthia Aviles-Landau individually, or in her capacity as administrator of the estate, or both. Furthermore, to the extent that Plaintiff's intent is to allege a cause of action for breach of contract, Plaintiff must properly label the cause of action and allege facts, not conclusions, supporting each element of the cause of action. Finally, Plaintiff is on notice that it cannot amend the complaint and add new causes of action after a demurrer is sustained with leave to amend without obtaining permission to do so. (Community Water Coalition v. Santa Cruz County Local Agency Formation Com. (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1317, 1329.)
The demurrer to the First Cause of Action for Receipt of Stolen Property and Extortion is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The property at issue, i.e., 331 Pope Street, was lawfully obtained through unlawful detainer proceedings, Case No. CUD-24-674073.
The demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for violation of Business & Professions Code 17200, is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff cannot show that "members of the public are likely to be deceived" based on the isolated incident that dealt with eviction proceedings. (See, Korea Supply Co. v, Lockheed Matin Corp. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1134, 1151.)
The demurrer to the Third Cause of Action for Breach of Fiduciary Duty is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff is not a beneficiary and therefore has no standing to bring the claim.
The demurrer to the Fourth Cause of Action for IIED is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff must allege facts, not conclusions, supporting each element of the cause of action.
To the extent that Plaintiff is alleging a claim for Misrepresentation in Count 5 and Count 6, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to plead with specificity, facts supporting each element of the cause of action.
Order signed. Judge: Victor M. Hwang ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
